deep_dive.title: Relational biological structure improves fine-mapping of causal GWAS variants under weak signal
deep_dive.abstract: Linkage disequilibrium (LD) makes causal GWAS variants indistinguishable from correlated neighbours; resolving them is the fine-mapping problem, and the challenge is species-specific: humans face dense ancestry-imbalanced LD, yeast and *Arabidopsis* exceptionally long LD, and crop germplasm sparse and fragmented annotations that defeat human-biobank curation pipelines. Bayesian fine-mappers integrate annotations as flat per-variant priors, discarding the relational structure linking variants to tissue-specific eQTLs, pathways and protein-protein interactions. Hierarchical belief propagation (HBP) on a variant-gene-pathway factor graph matches Bayesian baselines at 5-40x speed; an annotation-adaptive complement, graph-augmented fine-mapping (GAFM), wins 27-2 against SuSiE at weak signal and recovers *LDLR*, *APOE*, *LPL*, *GCKR* and *ANGPTL3* at single-variant resolution across four Pan-UK Biobank ancestries. On the 3,000 Rice Genomes grain weight + shape panel, mixture-prior posterior reweightings of GAFM/HBP and their ensemble (GAFM-MX, HBP-MX, ENS) reach 47.6% top-1-PIP exact-position recovery of 21 panel-matched stable QTNs - the highest of any method, exceeding SuSiE (28.6%) and SBayesRC (14.3%) - at 200-700x SuSiE's per-locus speed. Across 692 leads in four species, a non-uniform per-variant prior, not uniform high coverage, lets the graph break LD ties: adding a regulatory-element flag to an otherwise uniform human cache flips HBP narrower than GAFM from 0% to 88% on 321 Pan-UKB leads. These results recast multi-omics fine-mapping as a non-uniform-prior-curation problem rather than a uniform-coverage problem, and reframe post-GWAS analysis as message passing over biological structure rather than weighted regression on flattened annotations.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 利用关系生物学结构进行精细定位
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 利用关系生物学结构进行精细定位
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 利用关系生物学结构进行精细定位
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper introduces hierarchical belief propagation on a variant-gene-pathway factor graph for fine-mapping causal GWAS variants, achieving 5-40x speedup over Bayesian baselines while leveraging relational biological structure.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 连锁不平衡导致GWAS因果变异难以区分，精细定位是一大难题。现有贝叶斯方法将注释作为平坦先验，忽略了变异与组织特异eQTL、通路和蛋白质相互作用的关系结构。本研究提出在变异-基因-通路因子图上进行层次信念传播（HBP），在匹配贝叶斯基线准确率的同时实现5-40倍加速，并适应注释结构，显著改进了弱信号下的精细定位。
deep_dive.llm_tldr: 连锁不平衡导致GWAS因果变异难以区分，精细定位是一大难题。现有贝叶斯方法将注释作为平坦先验，忽略了变异与组织特异eQTL、通路和蛋白质相互作用的关系结构。本研究提出在变异-基因-通路因子图上进行层次信念传播（HBP），在匹配贝叶斯基线准确率的同时实现5-40倍加速，并适应注释结构，显著改进了弱信号下的精细定位。
deep_dive.matched_query_text: Statistical fine-mapping methods for causal variant identification

deep_dive.title: Is It You or Your Environment? A Bayesian Inference Framework for Genomically-Anchored Personalized Physiological Interpretation
deep_dive.abstract: Personalized health AI systems face a fundamental cold-start problem: machine learning models for physiological interpretation require weeks of individual behavioral data before they can distinguish constitutional variation from environmentally driven deviation. We propose a solution grounded in causal inference and Bayesian prior design. An individual's genomic profile serves as an exogenous genetic anchor -- a domain-informed, personalized prior that is fixed at conception, immune to reverse causation, and available before a single behavioral observation is collected. The anchor initializes a Bayesian belief state over an individual's physiological set point G-hat = mu + sum(beta_i * g_i), where beta_i are GWAS-derived effect sizes and g_i are risk-allele counts. Each incoming physiological measurement P produces a non-constitutional deviation delta = P - G-hat that separates the signal attributable to environment and state from the constitutionally fixed baseline. As behavioral data accrue, the prior decays according to G-hat_t = w(t)*G-hat_genomic + [1-w(t)]*P-bar_t, transitioning from genome-dominated to empirical-baseline-dominated inference. The same observed HRV of 55 ms generates a suppression hypothesis for a person whose prior predicts 80 ms, and an enhancement hypothesis for a person whose prior predicts 30 ms -- a reversal impossible without a personalized anchor. We develop this architecture across six physiological domains, grading genomic priors by evidence strength, distinguishing robustly replicated anchors (FTO, FADS1/2, FKBP5) from contested candidate genes (SLC6A4, MAOA, DRD2). We address the inference boundary between association, Mendelian randomization, and individual token causation, and define four constraints for deployment: evidence-graded priors, dynamic decay, ancestry-matched effect sizes, and attribution rather than deterministic output.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: genomic anchor as prior for personalized physiological interpretation, analogous to PRS calculation
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 基因组锚作为个性化生理解释的先验，类似PRS计算
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 基因组锚作为个性化生理解释的先验，类似PRS计算
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 基因组锚作为个性化生理解释的先验，类似PRS计算
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This paper uses genomic profile as a Bayesian prior to personalize physiological interpretation, directly relevant to PRS-based prediction and gene-environment interaction.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对个性化健康AI的冷启动问题，提出基于基因组锚的贝叶斯推断框架。利用个体基因组轮廓作为外生遗传锚定，构建个性化生理设定点的先验分布。该方法在观测数据收集前即可初始化，并区分遗传与环境贡献。该工作与多基因风险评分（PRS）的计算和预测建模高度相关，可用于提高PRS在个性化健康中的解释性。

deep_dive.title: Knowledge Graphs and Reasoning LLMs for Finding Simple Yet Effective Transcriptomic Perturbation Predictors
deep_dive.abstract: Predicting the effect of an unseen gene knockout perturbation on transcriptomic gene expression remains a highly challenging problem for virtual cell models. Recent progress has been made by leveraging biological knowledge graphs to provide a notion of similar perturbation, allowing for improved extrapolation beyond the set of training perturbations. In this work, we demonstrate that the simplest model to leverage these assumptions - a K-nearest neighbour from the knowledge graph - achieves highly competitive performance on this task, and that this can be improved further using LLMs optimised via reinforcement learning (RL) for predictive performance. Specifically, we find that the K-nearest neighbour approach beats almost all methods on out-of-distribution perturbation prediction, and when a reasoning LLM is trained via RL to make changes to the neighbourhood, it obtains equivalent performance to current state of the art methods on the cell lines from Replogle et al. (2022). We also demonstrate that the RL training improves the LLM's performance on the downstream task of differential expression prediction, despite not being trained on this directly. Overall, these findings demonstrate the efficacy of knowledge graphs as model priors, and show early signs that RL can refine LLMs into generalizable tools for predicting complex biological responses.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: knowledge graphs and RL-optimized LLMs for perturbation prediction
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 知识图谱和强化学习优化的大语言模型用于扰动预测
deep_dive.llm_evidence: 知识图谱和强化学习优化的大语言模型用于扰动预测
deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 知识图谱和强化学习优化的大语言模型用于扰动预测
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: This work shows that a simple K-nearest neighbor approach using biological knowledge graphs achieves competitive performance for transcriptomic perturbation prediction, and that RL-optimized LLMs can further improve predictive accuracy, advancing virtual cell models.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 预测未知基因敲除扰动对转录组基因表达的影响是虚拟细胞模型的一大挑战。本研究证明，利用生物学知识图的K近邻方法在此任务上具有高度竞争力，并通过强化学习（RL）优化的大语言模型进一步提升预测性能。该方法简单有效，为虚拟细胞建模和转录组扰动预测提供了新思路，体现了RL和LLM在基因组大模型中的应用。

deep_dive.title: inquiSTR: a toolkit for accurate and efficient population-scale tandem repeat genotyping and analysis
deep_dive.abstract: Tandem repeats are highly mutable genomic elements linked to human traits and diseases. Profiling large catalogs of tandem repeats from population-scale long-read sequencing data requires accurate and efficient tools. We introduce inquiSTR, a command-line toolkit for fast genome-wide tandem repeat length genotyping. inquiSTR, with efficient parallel processing and low-memory streaming algorithms, genotypes a genome-wide repeat catalog of 1.78 million loci in less than two minutes. Benchmarking shows high accuracy and significantly faster performance compared to existing tools and truth sets. inquiSTR also provides methods for downstream analyses such as population structure inference, association testing, and outlier detection.
deep_dive.llm_evidence_en: genome-wide tandem repeat genotyping with association testing
deep_dive.llm_evidence_cn: 全基因组串联重复基因分型与关联测试

deep_dive.canonical_evidence: 全基因组串联重复基因分型与关联测试
deep_dive.llm_tldr_en: Introduces inquiSTR, a fast toolkit for genome-wide tandem repeat genotyping and downstream analyses including association testing, enabling efficient repeat-based GWAS.
deep_dive.llm_tldr_cn: 串联重复序列是人类疾病相关的重要基因组元件，现有工具在群体规模数据处理上效率低下。本文提出了inquiSTR工具包，采用高效并行处理和低内存流式算法，可在两分钟内完成对178万个重复位点的全基因组基因分型。基准测试表明其准确性高且速度显著优于现有工具。该方法还支持群体结构推断、关联测试和异常值检测等下游分析，为重复序列全基因组关联研究提供了实用工具。

quick_skim.title: Enhancing Spectral Embedding through Robust and Flexible Knowledge Transfer in Electronic Health Records
quick_skim.abstract: We propose a spectral-based, unsupervised representation learning framework to derive low-dimensional embeddings for clinical concepts and patients in rare disease cohorts from electronic health records, where data are high-dimensional but sample sizes are limited. To overcome this challenge, we incorporate a knowledge matrix extracted from a broader population that shares a partially overlapping subspace with the rare-disease cohort. Our method departs from existing approaches by relaxing restrictive one-to-one signal-alignment assumptions between the latent data matrix and knowledge matrix, allowing more flexible and realistic forms of structured sharing. We introduce a novel two-step spectral embedding procedure: first, we identify and remove irrelevant components from the knowledge matrix; then, we apply a projection-based method to separately recover shared and heterogeneous components. Simulations and an analysis of a real-world multiple sclerosis cohort show that the proposed method outperforms competing approaches, particularly in challenging scenarios where shared signals are weak and only partially aligned, as is common in rare-disease data.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: unsupervised representation learning from EHR for rare diseases with knowledge transfer
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于电子健康记录的罕见病无监督表示学习与知识迁移

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a spectral embedding framework for rare disease EHR data, incorporating flexible knowledge transfer from a broader population to improve embeddings.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 从电子健康记录中学习低维表示在罕见病研究中面临样本量有限的问题。本文提出一种基于谱的无监督表示学习框架，通过引入来自更大群体的知识矩阵，允许灵活的跨域共享，避免了传统方法中严格的一对一信号对齐假设。该方法分两步进行谱嵌入，能够从高维稀疏数据中提取有意义的临床概念和患者表示。这对于缺乏足够数据的罕见病队列具有重要价值。

quick_skim.title: Learning quality scores for chromatin accessibility bigWig tracks using Machine Learning
quick_skim.abstract:
High-throughput chromatin accessibility assays such as bulk and single-cell ATAC-seq have generated large collections of processed signal tracks in bigWig format, which are widely used for visualisation, data integration, and Machine Learning (ML)-based analyses. Despite their central role, systematic quality control (QC) frameworks operating directly at the level of bigWig signal tracks remain underdeveloped. This gap limits the ability to assess data reliability and hampers robust downstream analyses.

Here, we present a biologically grounded QC framework for chromatin accessibility bigWig files that integrates peak-level information, background noise estimation, and recovery of stable genomic reference features. Using an ML-based peak caller (LanceOtron), we derive complementary quality metrics capturing signal structure and signal-to-noise properties. We further define constant promoter and CTCF regions as internal biological controls and show that their recovery provides a sensitive measure of data quality across diverse cellular contexts.

We apply this framework to a collection of 502 human chromatin accessibility bigWig tracks spanning a wide range of tissues and cell types. The proposed metrics capture related but non-redundant aspects of signal quality and motivate the use of constant promoter and CTCF recovery as biologically meaningful targets. An XGBoost model trained on LanceOtron-derived features accurately predicts recovery of these stable genomic elements on held-out data (R2 = 0.97), yielding a continuous and interpretable quality score. Feature importance analysis using SHAP values highlights that model decisions are driven by biologically relevant signal properties rather than arbitrary heuristics. Quantile-based stratification of the quality score is further supported by clear qualitative differences in genome browser visualisations.

Together, this work provides a principled and extensible frame-work for assessing the quality of chromatin accessibility bigWig tracks, enabling more reliable data integration and supporting downstream ML applications in regulatory genomics.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: ML-based QC framework for chromatin accessibility data, enabling robust integration with GWAS
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于机器学习的染色质可及性数据质控框架，为与GWAS整合奠定基础

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a biologically grounded QC framework for chromatin accessibility bigWig files using ML, integrating peak information, noise estimation, and reference feature recovery to improve data reliability for downstream analyses.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对染色质可及性bigWig轨道缺乏系统质控方法的问题，该文提出了一个基于机器学习的质控框架。该框架整合了峰级信息、背景噪声估计以及稳定基因组特征恢复，能够有效评估数据质量。实验表明，该方法可以显著提升下游分析（如数据整合和机器学习建模）的可靠性。该工作为功能基因组学数据的质量保证提供了重要工具，有助于推进与GWAS的整合分析。
